Union and Peru 11 December 2012 European Parliament approves the Agreement between Peru, Colombia and the European Union Source : European Commission's Directorate-General for Trade Implementation of the Agreement Article 15 of the Trade Agreement between the EU, Colombia and Peru provides for the establishment of several sub-committees. On February 13, 2014, Peru, Colombia and the European Union set up sub-committees on Technical Barriers to Trade; Agriculture; Intellectual Property and Trade and Sustainable Development. In addition, sub-committees on Market Access; Customs, Trade Facilitation and Rules of Origin; Government Procurement and Sanitary and Phytosanitary Measures will be established. The first meeting of the Trade Committee will take place in May. Documents relating to the Trade Committee 16 May 2014 Lima, Peru. Provisional application of EU-Ecuador agreement begins Contents Background and Negotiations EU-Ecuador EU-Colombia and Peru Documents relating to the Joint Committee EU-CAN Documents relating to the ad hoc working group Entry into Force EU-Ecuador EU-Colombia and Peru Implementation of the Agreement Related Articles and Studies Sources Related Pages Andean Countries-United Kingdom --- Andean Community-MERCOSUR Background and Negotiations On 16 April 1998, MERCOSUR members Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay and Uruguay, along with  Andean Community members Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru and Venezuela, signed a framework agreement towards the creation of a free trade agreement between the Andean Community and MERCOSUR. This agreement is registered with the ALADI Secretariat as agreement number AAP.A14TM. No.11. The agreement aims at a two-stage negotiation of a free trade agreement: in the first stage, lending on 30 September 1998, agreements on tariff preferences would be negotiated; then, between 01 October 1998 and 31 December 1999 negotiations towards a free trade agreement would take place. The negotiation of tariff preferences among the two groups began in June 1998. In 1999, however, at the request of Brazil, the process took two tracks:  one in which countries negotiated with Brazil; the other in which Andean countries negotiated with Argentina, Paraguay and Uruguay.   The negotiations between Brazil and the Andean Community concluded on 3 July 1999 and on 12 August 1999 an agreement was signed between Brazil and Colombia, Ecuador, Peru and Venezuela, registered with ALADI as Economic Complementation Agreement N � 39 (ACE39), which entered into force on 16 August 1999.  On 29 June 2000, Argentina signed a trade preferential agreement with Colombia, Ecuador, Peru and Venezuela,  which is registered with the ALADI as Economic Complementarity Agreement  N � 48 (ACE48) which, in conformity with Article 22 of that agreement, entered into force on 1 August 2000. Both of these agreements provide for their renewal upon agreement by all Parties, and will be replaced once the agreements towards the creation of a free trade agreement come into effect. Documents relating to the negotiations of tariff preferences between the Andean Community and the MERCOSUR 19 March 1999 Lima, Peru. Aide-m�moire of the second round of negotiations 16 April 1998 Framework agreement towards the creation of a free trade area between the Andean Community and MERCOSUR (AAP.A14TM No. 11) Documents relating to the negotiations of tariff preferences between the Andean Community and Brazil 12 August 1999 Economic Complementation Agreement N � 39 (ACE39) 03 July 1999 Official communiqu� regarding the Tariff Preference Agreement 26 June 1999 Communiqu� of the IV meeting 04 June 1999 Communiqu� of the III meeting 17 May 1999 Communiqu� of the II meeting 26 April 1999 Andean Community-Brazil Press Communiqu� Documents relating to the negotiations of tariff preferences between the Andean Community and Argentina 29 June 2000 Economic Complementation Agreement N � 48(ACE48) 31 March 2000 Act of the V meeting 10 March 2000 Act of the IV meeting 18 February 2000 Act of the III meeting 28 January 2000 Act of the II meeting 29 October 2000 Act initiating the Andean Community-Argentina negotiations towards a tariff preference agreement Source:  Secretaría General de la ALADI The second stage of the negotiations, towards the creation of a free trade area between the Andean Community and MERCOSUR, began in April 2001. On 6 December 2002, the Parties of the MERCOSUR and Member States of the Andean Community signed a framework agreement towards the formation of a free trade area. This agreement was registered with the ALADI Secretariat as Economic Complementation Agreement N �   56 (ACE56). Through this agreement, the Andean Community and MERCOSUR countries restated their intent to form a free trade area before 31 December 2003.  They also indicated that the free trade agreement agreement between Bolivia and MERCOSUR (this agreement, registered with the ALADI Secretariat as Economic Complementarity Agreement (ACE) N � 36, was signed on 17 December 1996) would remain in force. Documents relating to the negotiation of ACE 56 17 December 2003 Signing of the Andean-Community-MERCOSUR FTA 06 December 2003 Economic Complementation Agreement N � 56(ACE56) 22 November 2002 Rio de Janeiro, Brasil. Round I: Andean Community-MERCOSUR negotiations Source:  Ministerio de Comercio, Industria y Turismo, República de Colombia; Secretaría General de la ALADI In confor mity with the obligations set out in Economic Complementarity Agreement N � 56,  Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay and Uruguay (MERCOSUR Member States) and Colombia, Ecuador, Peru and Venezuela (Member states of the Andean Community) signed Economic Complementarity Agreement N � 59 on 18 October 2004. The objective of this agreement is to create a free trade area through the expansion and diversification of commercial exchange and the elimination of tariff and non-tariff barriers to reciprocal trade.  Peru concluded negotiation of a free trade agreement with MERCOSUR on 25 August 2003.  This agreement was signed on 30 November 2005 and registered with the ALADI Secretariat as Economic Complementarity Agreement N � 58. Cronolog�a de las negociaciones de la Comunidad Andina con el Mercosur Andean Community Secretariat.  Resumen del Acuerdo de Libre Comercio Colombia, Ecuador, Venezuela - Mercosur (ACE-59) Sources Centro de Economía Internacional (CEI), Ministerio de Relaciones Exteriores, Comercio Internacional y Culto de Argentina Ministerio de Comercio, Industria y Turismo de Colombia Secretar�a General de la ALADI Secretar�a General de la Comunidad Andina Recent Developments Developments related to Economic Complementation Agreement N � 36 (Bolivia-MERCOSUR) Developments related to Economic Complementation Agreement N � 58 (MERCOSUR-Peru) Developments related to Economic Complementation Agreement N � 59 (Colombia, Ecuador, Venezuela - MERCOSUR) Contents Background and Negotiations Related Articles and Studies Sources --- Andean Countries-United States Background and Negotiations On 18 November 2003, the USTR notified the U.S. Congress of the Administration's intent to enter into free trade negotiations with Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador and Peru, all of whom are beneficiaries of the Andean Trade Preference Act (ATPA). Negotiations were slated to begin in the second quarter of 2004, with Colombia and Peru. The United States and Colombia announced that they would begin free trade negotiations on 18 May 2004. They were joined by Peru and Ecuador. Bolivia participated as an observer.  On 7 December 2005 the United States and Peru announced that they had concluded negotiations. On 6 January the President sent to Congress a notice of his intent to enter into a free trade agreement with the Republic of Peru and on 12 April 2006 the United States and Peru signed the U.S.-Peru Trade Promotion Agreement (PTPA). On 27 February 2006, Colombia and the United States concluded their free trade agreement. A notice of intent to enter into a free trade agreement was sent to Congress on 24 August 2006. The Colombia-United Staes Trade Promotion Agreement was signed on 22 November 2006. XIV round of negotiations between Ecuador and the United States Contents Background and Negotiations Draft Texts Related Articles and Studies Sources Related TPD pages Colombia-United States Ecuador-United States Peru-United States --- Canada - Colombia Background and Negotiations On 31 May 1999, in Ottawa, Canada, the countries of the Andean Community -- Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru and Venezuela -- signed a Trade and Investment Cooperation Agreement (TICA) with the Government of Canada. On 7 August 2002 the Government of Canada announced the intention to begin preliminary talks with the members of the Andean Community on a possible free trade agreement. Canada, Colombia and Peru announced the launch of negotiations towards a free trade agreement on 7 June 2007. On 6 June 2008, after five rounds of negotiations, Canada and Colombia concluded the negotiations of a free trade agreement and labor and cooperation agreements. The Canada-Colombia Free Trade Agreement was signed on 21 November 2008. Canada and Colombia also signed a labour cooperation agreement and an agreement on the environment. Documents relating to the negotiations 21 November 2008 Canada and Colombia sign free trade agreement 07 June 2008 Bogota, Colombia. Canada and Colombia conclude negotiations for free trade, labor cooperation and environment agreements 07 March 2008 Miami, U.S. V round of Canada-Colombia free trade negotiations 04 December 2007 Lima, Peru. IV round of free trade negotiations Canada-Colombia-Peru 05 October 2007 Bogota, Colombia. III round of free trade negotiations Canada-Colombia-Peru 10 September 2007 Bogota, Colombia. II round of free trade negotiations Canada-Colombia-Peru 20 July 2007 Lima, Peru. I round of free trade negotiations Canada-Colombia 07 June 2007 Canada, Colombia and Peru announce launch of free trade negotiations 07 August 2002 Canada to begin talks on a proposed FTA with the Andean countries 31 May 1999 Trade and Investment Cooperation Arrangement between the Government of Canada and the Government of the Andean Community Source: Foreign Affairs and International Trade Canada; Ministerio de Comercio Exterior y Turismo, Perú; Ministerio de Comercio, Industria y Turismo, Colombia; Office of the Prime Minister, Canada Entry into Force In March 2009, the Government of Canada introduced legislation to implement the Canada-Peru and the Canada-Colombia Free Trade, Labour Cooperation and Environment Agreements in the House of Commons. In August 2009, the Canada-Colombia Free Trade Agreement was sent for the approval of the President of Colombia after it was approved by the Senate and the Chamber of Representatives of Colombia. On 10 March 2010, the Government of Canada tabled legislation to implement the Canada-Colombia Free Trade, Labour Cooperation and Environment Agreements in the House of Commons. On 30 June 2010, the Government of Canada announced that legislation to implement the Canada-Colombia FTA had received Royal Assent. On 15 August 2011, the Canada-Colombia Free Trade Agreement enters into force. Documents relating to entry into force 15 August 2011 The Canada-Colombia FTA enters into force 10 August 2011 The Canada-Colombia FTA will enter into force on 15 Augus t   30 June 2010 Legislation to implement Canada’s free trade agreement with Colombia has received Royal Assent 10 March 2010 Government of Canada tabled legislation to implement the Canada-Colombia FTA and related agreements in the House of Commons 11 August 2009 FTA sent to President of Colombia for approval 26 March 2009 Canada introduces legislation to implement Colombia and Peru Free Trade Agreements Implementation of the Agreement Article 2001of the Canada-Colombia Free Trade Agreement establishes the Joint Commission, made up of cabinet-level representatives, to supervise the implementation of the Agreement, oversee its further elaboration, review its general functioning, supervise the work of the committees, working groups, and country coordinators as well as to consider any other matters that may affect the operation of the Agreement. The first meeting of the Free Trade Joint Commission took place in Ottawa, Canada, on 28 February 2014. Annual Report Pursuant to the Agreement Concerning Annual Reports on Human Rights and Free Trade between Canada and the Republic of Colombia Contents Background and Negotiations Entry into Force Implementation of Agreement Related Articles and Studies Sources --- Canada - Peru Background and Negotiations On 31 May 1999, in Ottawa, Canada, the countries of the Andean Community -- Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru and Venezuela -- signed a Trade and Investment Cooperation Agreement (TICA) with the Government of Canada. On 7 August 2002, the Government of Canada announced the intention to begin preliminary talks with the members of the Andean Community on a possible free trade agreement. Canada, Colombia and Peru announced the launch of negotiations towards a free trade agreement on 7 June 2007. On 26 January 2008, after three rounds of negotiations, Canada and Peru concluded their FTA. On 29 May 2008, Canada and Peru sign a free trade agreement after ten months of negotiations. In March 2009, the Government of Canada introduced legislation to implement the Canada-Peru and the Canada-Colombia Free Trade, Labour Cooperation and Environment Agreements in the House of Commons. On 18 June 2009, the Government of Canada announced that legislation to implement Canada’s free trade agreement with Peru had been adopted by the Parliament and received Royal Assent. The Government also announced that it would work to have its FTA with Peru, including parallel agreements on labour co-operation and environment, implemented by as early as July 1 st . The Free Trade Agreement (FTA) between Canada and Peru came into force on August 1 st , 2009. Documents relating to the negotiations August Canada - Peru FTA comes into effect  18 June 2009 Legislation to implement Canada’s free trade agreement with Peru was adopted by the Parliament and received Royal Assent 26 March 2009 Canada introduces legislation to implement Colombia and Peru Free Trade Agreements 29 May 2008 Lima, Peru.
